engine.measurement_policy: voice-quality metrics restricted to sustained voiced notes; deterministic scoring rubric v1

pitch.range_semitones_note: Robust range (2.5th-97.5th percentile of voiced frames). Immune to single octave-error frames.

voice_quality.cpps_note: Cepstral Peak Prominence (smoothed, Praat). Research-standard clarity measure; diagnostic only until the reference pack is re-analysed with it.

strain.method: high pitch + above-median loudness + HNR >=4 dB below this take's own median
strain.reliability: medium — heuristic; intentional grit also trips it, confirm by ear at the timestamps

voice_quality.interpretation: Clean, well-supported phonation; normal sung-note perturbation; elevated amplitude perturbation.

reference.note: Speech norms are for sustained spoken vowels; sung notes with vibrato run naturally higher. Interpretation above uses singing-oriented bands.

harmonic_balance.note: Global tonal-balance descriptor over the whole file (includes silence/consonants). NOT clinical HNR — see voice_quality.hnr_db_median.

resonance.singers_formant_note: Median 2-4 kHz vs 80 Hz-2 kHz energy on active frames. Heuristic bands; diagnostic until pro-pack anchors exist.

dynamics.reference_note: dB relative to the loudest frame in this file (internal contrast, not absolute loudness).

rhythm.tempo_confidence: low (isolated vocal stem — beat tracking expects a full mix)

vowel_space.reliability: medium — vowel identity from F1/F2 is approximate on sung (vs spoken) vowels; excluded above F0 350 Hz where formant tracking breaks down

vibrato.onset_delay_note: Time from note start until vibrato blooms. Pros typically ~0.2-0.6 s; near-zero can read as nervous wobble, very late as an afterthought.

intonation.caveat: Grid-deviation is a proxy: expressive slides, blue notes and non-12-TET material register as deviation. Interpret alongside the recording context.

registers.reliability: medium — verify by ear at the timestamps

breath.method: pitch slope over the final 0.5 s of each phrase
breath.note: Sagging endings on LONG phrases suggest breath running out; on short phrases, intentional fall-offs are common in rock/soul — judge by ear.

range_map.note: Time-weighted from this take only. Compare across takes/songs for a true personal range picture.

onsets.method: first 0.25 s of each sustained note vs its settled centre
onsets.note: Scooping can be deliberate style — consistency matters more than the raw percentage. Compare with the original via tools/compare_takes.py to see whether the original scoops in the same places.

harmonics.method: per-sustained-note FFT, harmonic peaks at k*F0, median across notes
harmonics.note: Numeric overtone profile. On separated stems, separation artifacts add noise to upper harmonics — read H5+ cautiously.

groove.method: vocal onsets vs half-beat grid of the instrumental stem
groove.note: Negative = ahead of the beat. Persistent |offset| under ~25 ms reads as tight; deliberate back-phrasing (soul/jazz) shows as consistent positive offsets — style, not error.

problem_zones.note: Low reliable vocal pitch detection in this window.
F0 varied heavily in this 10-second window. Treat cautiously if backing vocals or room noise are present.
F0 varied heavily in this 10-second window. Treat cautiously if backing vocals or room noise are present.
Strong forward/bright energy. Useful for cut, but can also include room bleed or harsh mic capture.

environment_risk.note: No obvious global capture-risk marker was detected.

visual_diagnostics.note: Supporting visual diagnostics. In karaoke/live-room recordings, backing track bleed and mic clipping can affect these traces.

technical_score.capture_fair_note: Rubric with voice_quality excluded (weights renormalised). Jitter/shimmer/HNR partly measure the recording chain: vintage masters run through stem separation read far worse than clean modern captures of an equal voice. Use capture_fair on BOTH sides for any take-vs-original or cross-era comparison; use overall_score for a singer's absolute result and self-progress.
technical_score.provenance: deterministic_rubric_v2 — computed from measured audio features; identical audio yields an identical score; no LLM involvement; theoretical anchors (no pro calibration file found)

calibration.note: Run tools/build_calibration.py over analyses of 15-20 professional reference takes to anchor 9-10 to the professional distribution.

intonation_accuracy.formula: 10 at 5 cents or better, 0 at 45 cents, linear (uncalibrated anchors)

pitch_stability.formula: 10 at 10 cents or better, 0 at 80 cents, linear (uncalibrated anchors)

voice_quality.formula: mean of jitter/shimmer/HNR sub-scores (Praat, per sustained note)

vibrato_control.input: 47.6% of long notes carry vibrato (10 at >=40%); rate 5.87 Hz (ideal 5.0-7.0); extent 67.0 cents (ideal 25.0-130.0); intra-note drift 28.9 cents (straight-tone path)
vibrato_control.formula: best of vibrato-quality path and straight-tone steadiness path (consistent straight tone is a valid professional style)

dynamics_expression.input: phrase-level spread 23.55 dB (ideal 3.0-12.0); effective range 47.03 dB (ideal 6.0-22.0)
dynamics_expression.formula: best of phrase-level shaping and effective range (mastered/compressed stems limit raw range through no fault of the singer)

phrase_control.formula: 10 at 2.5 s or better, 0 at 0.5 s, linear (uncalibrated anchors)

technical_score.scope_note: Technical execution only. Artistry, emotion, interpretation and style-appropriateness are human judgements and are not part of this number. Any 'listener impact' figure elsewhere in a report is a subjective estimate, not a measurement.

prescriptions.method: deterministic_prescription_engine_v1 — rules over measured values; exercise content verbatim from the library; no LLM selection

primary.selector_heading: If the main limiter is BREATH / SUPPORT
evidence: 34.8% of phrase endings sag (intentional fall-offs also register — confirm by ear)
primary.primary_exercise_detail:
- Use when: suspended breath control and phrase stamina.
- Pedagogical target: Suspended Breath Control.
- How to do it: Inhale for 4 counts, suspend (hold with open glottis) for 4, exhale for 4; increase to 10+ counts.
- How it should feel: Inhale low and wide, suspend without closing the throat, then release like a slow leak.
- Common failure / safety note: Stop or reduce intensity if dizziness, shoulder lifting, throat gripping, or breath-holding appears.
- Pass/Fail metric: Counts are even and the throat stays open during the suspension.
- Song transfer: Apply the same sensation to one short phrase only; do not run the whole song until the cue holds.
primary.next_take_cue: Keep the ribs wide and let the air meter out slowly.

prescriptions.note: Primary drill = highest-severity measured limiter. Candi may choose among the category's listed exercises using drill history, or justify a deviation in prose.

diagnostic_flags.interpretation: Strong forward placement. Good projection and cut.
diagnostic_flags.likely_cause: Aryepiglottic twang / raised larynx / wide oral cavity.
diagnostic_flags.intervention: Maintain — no correction needed. Monitor for laryngeal elevation.
